actions.description: Actions the UI may offer when the capability is not dependable.

available.description: Whether the capability can be relied on right now. Mirrors `maturity.is_dependable()` for the baseline but is carried explicitly so a live report can mark a dependable capability temporarily unavailable (e.g. unhealthy provider) without changing its maturity.

evidence_refs.description: Evidence backing the maturity claim (provider/adapter/contract refs).

reason_code.description: Stable machine reason for the current maturity/availability.

title.description: Short human-facing surface name, e.g. `Chat code edit`.

CapabilityMaturity.description: How real a capability is at runtime. Ordered from most to least dependable.

oneOf.description: A real, end-to-end implementation whose user-visible action performs the real side effect. Requires non-empty `evidence_refs`.
Live but operating with a known limitation (e.g. reduced concurrency, a legacy transport still in the path). Requires non-empty `evidence_refs`.
Code/types/persistence exist but the vertical path is not yet wired to a real side effect. Must not be shown as live.
Deterministic stand-in that mimics success without doing the real work. Must be visually distinct from live in the UI.
Not implemented or not currently usable (missing setup/credentials).
